title: lupinus perennis var. gracilis
intro: Lupinus perennis var. gracilis, also known as the woodland lupine or narrow-leaved lupine, is a variety of the perennial lupine plant, native to the eastern United States and parts of Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the alkaloids present in the plant. The health benefits of Lupinus perennis var. gracilis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
